Chronology of every recorded fact about [[Monarch]], in reading order. Generated from the same verified fact store as the main article.

== Skysworn ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|'''Ch 11''' — Monarchs are practically myths.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|'''Ch 16''' — A Monarch can turn the Dreadgod aside.}}

== Ghostwater ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|'''Ch 6''' — Lindon is trapped in a Monarch's pocket world.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|'''Ch 13''' — A Monarch can invent a new technique very frequently.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|'''Ch 19''' — Monarchs have children who enjoy resources like Spirit Wells.}}

== Underlord ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|'''Ch 3''' — A Monarch has a home with fountains and gardens.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|'''Ch 3''' — The Monarch is a figure mentioned in relation to Mercy's family history.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|'''Ch 8''' — A substance that strengthens the soul might be prepared in a Monarch’s pocket world.}}

== Uncrowned ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|'''Ch 2''' — A Monarch's hand can cause blood madra to splatter like a spray.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|'''Ch 13''' — The Monarch provides infallible protection to competitors in the arena.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|'''Ch 17''' — The Monarch spoke to someone.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|'''Ch 18''' — The Monarch restored Yerin to pristine condition.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|'''Ch 19''' — A Monarch can immediately resurrect Yerin.}}

== Wintersteel ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|'''Ch 2''' — Every person in the audience hall of the Ninecloud Court besides Lindon was a Monarch.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|'''Ch 3''' — Monarchs have the ability to hear their name spoken.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|'''Ch 3''' — Northstrider aims to become a Monarch.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|'''Ch 3''' — The birth of a Monarch causes reality to quake and aura to tremble.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|'''Ch 3''' — The nature of Monarchs is a secret for anyone below Archlord.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|'''Ch 7''' — The Monarch can take anything he wants without trickery.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|'''Ch 7''' — Monarchs are capable of traveling great distances at a thought.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|'''Ch 8''' — The Monarch expects great things from Mercy.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|'''Ch 8''' — Monarchs can command reality directly.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|'''Ch 11''' — Monarch factions do not spend advancement resources lightly.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|'''Ch 14''' — Information from the Monarch is as valuable as any technique library.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|'''Ch 15''' — A Monarch is a high level of power; the tournament's start was ordered by one.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|'''Ch 17''' — An enemy Monarch could obtain a weapon of heavenly death.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|'''Ch 18''' — A Monarch can be killed.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|'''Ch 19''' — Monarch is a path or rank that can be achieved through unique opportunities.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|'''Ch 25''' — A Monarch can transport people to opulent display halls.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|'''Ch 26''' — Fury is ascending to the Monarch stage.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|'''Ch 28''' — A Monarch died unceremoniously.}}

== Bloodline ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|'''Ch 2''' — A Monarch is a threat.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|'''Ch 5''' — Monarch is a rank or title held by Tiberian Arelius.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|'''Ch 17''' — The Kazan clan traveled during a fight between the Wandering Titan and a Monarch.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|'''Ch 17''' — The Monarch is a being Lindon expected would circle the suppression field.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|'''Ch 22''' — The Sage was granted a tool by a Monarch.}}

== Reaper ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 3''' — A Monarch can provide protection to Sages and Heralds.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 4''' — Eithan may have bartered with the Monarch.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 4''' — Monarchs are powerful beings that might eavesdrop on conversations.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 4''' — The Divine Treasure was created by a Monarch.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 4''' — A Monarch can deactivate a cloud regardless of scripts.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 5''' — Monarchs fought on Naru Huan's western border.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 7''' — Advancing to Monarch is the goal for a Herald who has reached the peak.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 13''' — A Monarch's Remnant is a type of Remnant that can be tempered in Archlord soulfire.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 13''' — Forging a sword for a Monarch would ideally be done on a battlefield where Monarchs died.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 14''' — Ozmanthus' failed Penance prototypes could have graced a Monarch’s armory.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 19''' — The group fought a Monarch.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 20''' — Eithan mentions punching the Monarch.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 22''' — Lindon wonders if the Monarch has found his way back.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 25''' — Monarchs keep each other in check to prevent the number of Monarchs from becoming too high.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 25''' — A Monarch can make people swear to keep their mouths shut.}}

== Dreadgod ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 3''' — Pride tells Lindon he will reach Monarch eventually if no one kills him.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 3''' — A Monarch cannot spy on Lindon within the labyrinth without his permission.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 3''' — Most Monarchs have never set foot in the labyrinth.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 3''' — Others must be brought to the level of fighting Dreadgods and Monarchs.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 5''' — A Monarch used a formation array to freeze a citadel in the Wastelands.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 6''' — A Monarch can command others with a sense of compulsion.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 18''' — Fighting the Silent King is like facing four Monarchs at once.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 19''' — Reigan Shen is referred to as a Monarch.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 21''' — Monarchs can compress time into pocket worlds.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 21''' — Monarchs can combine their authority to stabilize a spirit.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 23''' — A Monarch can hold Akura armor through more stress than a Truegold.}}

== Waybound ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 3''' — Lindon tells Orthos and Little Blue they won't make it to Monarch in the time remaining.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 8''' — A Monarch is someone who can tell the world how it works.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 9''' — The clouds overhead were signs of a furious Monarch.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 9''' — A Monarch can use a thunderbolt attack.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 10''' — Monarchs and Dreadgods are opponents that cannot be exhausted or outlasted.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 12''' — A Monarch can use many techniques in a minute.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 15''' — Larian becomes a Monarch by drawing power from her comrades.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 15''' — Mercy feels like she could dodge a Monarch.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 20''' — Dross suggests Lindon should have been born to a Monarch based on the quality of Mercy's vessel.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 24''' — The Phoenix suggests setting up Monarchs of their own.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 25''' — The Book of Eternal Night must end at Monarch.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 25''' — A Monarch's will was used to haul Lindon through space via the horn.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 26''' — To rule as a Monarch requires making difficult decisions.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 29''' — Monarch is a stage of advancement.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 29''' — The Endless Sword technique is powered by a Monarch.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 30''' — Dross mentions Monarchs on Cradle.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 36''' — The people in the new world were bound to powers like veiled Monarchs.}}

== Threshold: Stories from Cradle ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|'''Ch 1''' — Monarchs use spatial manipulation.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|'''Ch 1''' — Monarchs command the world.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|'''Ch 6''' — Monarchs are known to be capable of tearing open spatial rifts.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|'''Ch 13''' — The Monarch can decide to feed Yerin's town.}}

[[Category:Timelines]]
